(skin) that grows in from the edges or as islands on the ulcer surface. 02 = Granulation tissue: Pink or beefy red tissue with a shiny, moist granular appearance, 03 = Slough: Yellow or white tissue that adheres to the ulcer bed in strings or thick clumps or is mucinous, 04 = Necrotic tissue (eschar): Black, brown, or tan tissue that adheres firmly to the wound bed or ulcer edges.
